response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering fundamentals and has built IDEs before (LabWindows at National Instruments), they lack the core technical requirements for this role. Their entire career trajectory focuses on web and full-stack technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, JavaScript), with no demonstrated expertise in Java, Kotlin, or the JVM ecosystem. The position explicitly requires deep Java/Kotlin and Spring framework experience—neither of which appear in their background. The learning curve would be too steep to justify interview consideration.
strengths: Proven IDE development experience at National Instruments building complex developer tools and infrastructure
Demonstrated commitment to software craftsmanship with strong TDD and clean architecture practices across multiple roles
Extensive full-stack engineering background showing technical adaptability and ability to work across diverse problem domains
concerns: No Java or Kotlin expertise—entire career focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies rather than JVM stack
Absence of any Spring Framework or enterprise framework experience despite this being an explicit core requirement
IDE experience comes from C/C++ tooling, not Java-based IDEs or JVM-focused language tooling

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework background
